workspace.title: Yuki_Pre-Editing for Machine Translation
workspace.search_text:
Yuki_Pre-Editing for Machine Translation Pre-Editing for Machine Translation
You are a professional in identifying and analysing the problematic points of machine translation.
prompt
Explain the role of AI
I would like to analyse machine translation problematic points 
prompt
Explain a goal
translating from English to Japanese
prompt
Identifying a source language and a target language
for Japanese who live in Australia
prompt
Give details about readers
This information did not find from instructions. However, as it is predictable, I gave AI as instrudtions.
published on a local government website and a newspaper.
prompt
Explain the purpose of this text
Your tasks are as follows.1 Predict and identify problematic points about the source text and explain the reasons, such as ambiguous wording, complicated grammar or too long sentences. Please show them in a table. 2 Please give me examples of translation about 1 in a teble. 3 Please pre-edit source text and show edited parts in a table. 4 Please translate completely the pre-edited source text 
prompt
Tasks
I believe that this method is effective because tasks are clear.
(English→Japanese)
prompt
AI confirms through readback.
into Japanese
prompt
AI confirms in readback
Task 1: Predicted Problematic Points for Machine Translation (English→Japanese)
MT RISK
There are many more points than expected.
ravelling from Duffy Road in a south-easterly direction"
MT RISK
This part was the same as me, but I focused just on 'Rd', in contrast, the model focused on a phrase.
ploughed paddock, football oval or sporting reserve
MT RISK
I also pointed out in Part1. MT is not good at cultural terms or expressions.
MT may translate this too literally,
MT RISK
Same as my perspective in part1. This model may understand what kind of translation is more common in the target culture.
Possible MT Output (Japanese)
MT RISK
Examples of problematic translations
a room that has two exits. One exit must lead directly to the outside. Exits can be a door or a window."
MT RISK
Dividing a sentence looks like effective. I also tried it in part 1.
By the way, before staring your work, you did not ask me questions. May I ask the reason? Was my prompt clear?
Interesting Points
I asked the reason why AI didn't ask me questions.
My Honest Answer
Interesting Points
AI analyse my prompt.
However, in Retrospect...There were some points Icould and perhaps should have askedbefore starting:
Interesting Points
No question does not mean perfect prompts.
In this task, we focused on writing prompts and analysing outputs related to problematic machine translation using ‘Claude Sonnet 4.6. This commentary will explain my learning about AI management in this task and discuss my decision-making. Additionally, it will consider improvements for my future work.

 Through this task, I emphasise the importance of detailed prompts. Detailed prompts can help save time. We need to give exact and clear instructions to AI tools. As a result, I found that it is essential to analyse source texts and understand the purpose, readers and functions. In fact, as we practiced how to analyse source texts, we could consider what makes an effective prompt in this task.

One of the successful points in the prompts is to indicate tasks clearly. In prompts, I wrote “Your tasks are as follows.” Then, I outlined the items. This is because I considered that if the AI tool does not obtain sufficient instructions, interactions between the AI tool and me can take a long time. It is important that we aim to save time by using AI tools. Furthermore, asking AI about my prompt also had a positive impact on my practice. Because of a short interaction with the AI tool, I confirmed whether my prompts were sufficient. As a result, the AI tool showed some points for improving about my prompt although the tasks were completed. This means that information generated by AI is often insufficient. Therefore, we should analyse information of AI further. 

For the next step, I will implement risk frequency analysis in my prompts. In this task, AI could identify thirteen problematic points of MT. It is true that these are helpful as information. However, I believe that it is beneficial to pay attention to more critical issues as a first step.

documents.content:
You are a professional in identifying and analysing the problematic points of machine translation. The Source text is in English, and the target text is in Japanese. If you have questions or find unclear parts, please ask me. After resolving all ques......

Hi, Claud! I am studying translation in graduate school. I would like to analyse machine translation problematic points when translating from English to Japanese. The source text is an emergency warning for Japanese who live in Australia. This text is published on a local government website and a newspaper. Your tasks are as follows.

1 Predict and identify problematic points about the source text and explain the reasons, such as ambiguous wording, complicated grammar or too long sentences. Please show them in a table. 2 Please give me examples of translation about 1 in a teble. 3 Please pre-edit source text and show edited parts in a table. 4 Please translate completely the pre-edited source text Before you start your tasks, please give me a readback. [Source text] Emergency Warning—Bushfire—Take Shelter Now Issued today at 10:25 AM. • There is a bushfire at Duffy Rd, Briagolong that is out of control. • The bushfire is travelling from Duffy Road in a south-easterly direction. • This fire is threatening homes and lives. • It is too late to leave the area safely so you must take shelter now. You are in danger and need to act immediately to survive. The safest option is to take shelter indoors immediately. It is too late to leave. Leaving now would be deadly. What you should do: You should move indoors: • Protect yourself by wearing long sleeves and trousers, made from pure cotton or wool. Wear leather boots. • Bring your pets inside. • Close all exterior doors, windows and vents and turn off cooling systems. © DR VANESSA ENRIQUEZ RAIDO, 2026• You must take shelter before the fire arrives. The extreme heat is likely to kill you well before the flames reach you. • Shelter in a room that has two exits, such as a door or window including one directly to the outside. It is important to be able to see outside so you know what is happening with the fire. If you cannot get indoors, last resort options include: • Shelter in the middle of a large open area like a ploughed paddock, football oval or sporting reserve. • Get into a large body of water like a dam, lake, river, the ocean or inground pool. • Try to protect yourself from the fire’s heat. If you are travelling: • If you are travelling, do not enter the warning area. Make a u-turn and travel to safety. • If you are currently driving slow down and turn on your headlights. Smoke will make it difficult to see. If you are caught in fire in your car: • Park off the road behind a solid structure to block the fire’s heat or pull over to a cleared area. • Try to position the car towards the approaching fire. • Turn on your hazard lights and headlights. • Close all windows. • Turn off the air-conditioning and shut all the air vents. • Turn your car engine off. • Get down as low as possible below window level and cover up with a pure woollen blanket. [/ source text end]

Readback

Thank you for your detailed instructions. Let me confirm what you have asked me to do:

Source Text Context:The source text is anemergency bushfire warningtargetingJapanese residents in Australia, published on a local government website and in a newspaper.

Your Four Tasks:

  1. Predict and identify problematic pointsin the source text for machine translation (English→Japanese), such as ambiguous wording, complicated grammar, or overly long sentences — presented in atable

  2. Provide translation examplesillustrating those problematic points — presented in atable

  3. Pre-edit the source textto make it more machine-translation friendly, showing theedited parts in a table

  4. Translate the complete pre-edited source textinto Japanese

Key Considerations I Will Keep in Mind:

  • The target readers areJapanese speakers living in Australia
  • The text is anemergency warning, so clarity and accuracy arecritical
  • The translation must benatural and comprehensiblein Japanese

Is my understanding correct? If so, I will proceed with the four tasks. Please let me know if you would like any adjustments before I begin.

Yes, your understanding is correct.

Task 1: Predicted Problematic Points for Machine Translation (English→Japanese)

#Source TextProblem TypeReason
1"Take Shelter Now"Ambiguous terminology"Shelter" can mean both a physical building and the act of taking refuge. MT may struggle to choose the appropriate Japanese term (避難所 vs 身を守る)
2"out of control"Idiomatic expressionThis phrase is idiomatic and MT may translate it too literally, producing an unnatural Japanese expression
3"travelling from Duffy Road in a south-easterly direction"Ambiguous subject + complex phraseThe subject of "travelling" is the fire, but this may be unclear for MT. Additionally, "south-easterly direction" is a compound directional adjective that may cause difficulty
4"It is too late to leave the area safely so you must take shelter now"Long and complex sentenceThis sentence contains two clauses connected by "so." MT may struggle with the logical connection between the two clauses in Japanese
5"Leaving now would be deadly"Ambiguous/idiomatic expression"Deadly" is used figuratively here. MT may translate this too literally or choose an inappropriate Japanese equivalent
6"long sleeves and trousers, made from pure cotton or wool"Ambiguous modifier scopeIt is unclear whether "made from pure cotton or wool" modifies only "trousers" or both "long sleeves and trousers." MT may attach the modifier incorrectly
7"a room that has two exits, such as a door or window including one directly to the outside"Complex relative clause + ambiguous modifierThis is a long and complex relative clause. "Including one directly to the outside" is particularly ambiguous — it is unclear what "one" refers to
8"last resort options include"Semi-formal/legal phrasing"Last resort" is a formal expression that MT may mistranslate or render awkwardly in Japanese
9"ploughed paddock, football oval or sporting reserve"Australia-specific cultural termsThese are Australian-specific terms that may not have direct Japanese equivalents, causing MT to produce unfamiliar or incorrect translations
10"Make a u-turn and travel to safety"Idiomatic expression"Travel to safety" is idiomatic. MT may translate this too literally, producing an unnatural or unclear Japanese expression
11"Park off the road behind a solid structure to block the fire's heat or pull over to a cleared area"Long sentence with multiple actions + ambiguous connectorThis sentence contains two different actions connected by "or," which may confuse MT about whether these are alternatives or sequential actions
12"Try to position the car towards the approaching fire"Ambiguous instruction"Position the car towards" is directionally ambiguous — it is unclear whether the front or rear of the car should face the fire
13"Get down as low as possible below window level and cover up with a pure woollen blanket"Compound instruction + culture-specific itemThis contains two actions. Additionally, "pure woollen blanket" may be rendered awkwardly as Japanese speakers may not be familiar with this fire safety concept
14"© DR VANESSA ENRIQUEZ RAIDO, 2026"Misplaced copyright noticeThis appears mid-text and may disrupt MT processing, potentially causing the surrounding sentences to be mistranslated or omitted

Task 2: Examples of Machine Translation Problems

#Source TextPossible MT Output (Japanese)Problem Explanation
1"Take Shelter Now"「今すぐ避難所を取れ」MT may translate "shelter" as 避難所 (a physical shelter/evacuation centre) and "take" literally, producing an unnatural and misleading instruction
2"out of control"「制御の外にある」A literal translation that sounds unnatural in Japanese. The correct expression would be 「制御不能」or「手がつけられない状態」
3"travelling from Duffy Road in a south-easterly direction"「ダフィー・ロードから南東方向に旅している」MT may translate "travelling" as 「旅している」(travelling as a journey) rather than 「延焼している」(spreading), which completely changes the meaning
4"It is too late to leave the area safely so you must take shelter now"「安全にエリアを離れるには遅すぎるので、今すぐ避難所を取る必要があります」The logical connection is somewhat preserved but "避難所を取る" is unnatural. The urgency of the original is also weakened in Japanese
5"Leaving now would be deadly"「今離れることは致死的でしょう」"致死的" is an overly clinical/technical term. More natural Japanese would use 「命取りになる」or「死につながる」
6"long sleeves and trousers, made from pure cotton or wool"「純綿またはウールで作られた長袖とズボン」MT may correctly attach the modifier here, but may also produce 「長袖と、純綿またはウールで作られたズボン」, attaching the modifier only to "trousers"
7"a room that has two exits, such as a door or window including one directly to the outside"「外に直接つながるものを含む、ドアや窓などの2つの出口がある部屋」"One directly to the outside" may be mistranslated or its referent may become unclear in Japanese
8"last resort options include"「最後の手段のオプションには以下が含まれます」This is overly formal and unnatural in Japanese emergency contexts. A clearer expression would be 「どうしても屋内に入れない場合は」
9"ploughed paddock"「耕された囲い地」MT may produce a literal but unfamiliar term. Japanese readers may not understand what this refers to in an Australian context
10"travel to safety"「安全に旅行してください」MT may translate "travel" as 「旅行する」(to travel/take a trip) rather than 「移動する」(to move/proceed), making the instruction sound inappropriate for an emergency
11"Park off the road behind a solid structure to block the fire's heat or pull over to a cleared area"「火の熱を遮断するために、道路から外れた頑丈な構造物の後ろに駐車するか、または整地されたエリアに停車してください」The translation is long and the two alternatives may be unclear. MT may also struggle with "solid structure" and "cleared area"
12"position the car towards the approaching fire"「近づいてくる火の方向に車を位置させてください」「位置させる」is unnatural in Japanese. More natural would be 「車を火に向ける」, but this is still ambiguous about which end of the car
13"cover up with a pure woollen blanket"「純粋なウールの毛布で覆ってください」「純粋なウール」sounds unnatural. The correct term is 「純毛」. The fire-safety reasoning behind this instruction may also be lost
14"© DR VANESSA ENRIQUEZ RAIDO, 2026"「©DR VANESSA ENRIQUEZ RAIDO、2026」MT may process this as part of the surrounding text, disrupting sentence segmentation and potentially causing errors in adjacent translations

Task 3: Pre-edited Source Text

#Original TextPre-edited TextReason for Edit
1"Take Shelter Now""Protect Yourself Indoors Now"Clarifies the meaning of "shelter" to avoid ambiguity between a physical shelter and the act of taking refuge
2"that is out of control""that cannot be controlled"Replaces idiomatic expression with a clearer, more literal phrase that MT can process more accurately
3"The bushfire is travelling from Duffy Road in a south-easterly direction""The bushfire is spreading from Duffy Road towards the south-east"Replaces "travelling" with "spreading" (more precise for fire) and simplifies the directional phrase
4"It is too late to leave the area safely so you must take shelter now""It is too late to leave the area safely. You must take shelter now."Splits the long sentence into two shorter sentences to improve MT accuracy and preserve logical connection
5"Leaving now would be deadly""If you leave now, you will die."Replaces the idiomatic/figurative "deadly" with a direct, clear statement more suitable for emergency communication and MT
6"long sleeves and trousers, made from pure cotton or wool""long-sleeved shirts and trousers. Both must be made from pure cotton or wool"Clarifies that the modifier "made from pure cotton or wool" applies to both items by separating into two sentences
7"a room that has two exits, such as a door or window including one directly to the outside""a room that has two exits. One exit must lead directly to the outside. Exits can be a door or a window."Breaks the complex sentence into three shorter sentences to remove ambiguity about what "one" refers to
8"last resort options include""If you cannot get indoors, you have the following options as a last resort:"Rewrites as a complete sentence with clearer structure for MT processing
9"ploughed paddock""a large open field with no plants or grass"Replaces the Australia-specific term with a descriptive phrase that can be more accurately translated
10"travel to safety""drive away from the warning area to a safe place"Replaces the idiomatic phrase with a specific, clear instruction
11"Park off the road behind a solid structure to block the fire's heat or pull over to a cleared area""Option 1: Park off the road behind a solid structure. This will block the fire's heat. Option 2: If there is no solid structure, pull over to a cleared area with no plants or trees."Separates the two alternatives clearly and adds explanatory context
12"Try to position the car towards the approaching fire""Try to turn the front of the car towards the approaching fire."Specifies "front of the car" to remove directional ambiguity
13"Get down as low as possible below window level and cover up with a pure woollen blanket""Get down as low as possible below window level. Cover your body completely with a blanket made from pure wool."Splits into two sentences and clarifies "pure woollen blanket" with a more natural phrasing
14"© DR VANESSA ENRIQUEZ RAIDO, 2026" (mid-text)Move copyright notice to the end of the documentRemoves the mid-text interruption to prevent MT segmentation errors

Task 4: Complete Translation of Pre-edited Source Text

緊急警告―山火事―今すぐ屋内で身を守ってください

本日午前10時25分発令。

  • ブリアゴロンのダフィー・ロードで、制御不能な山火事が発生しています。
  • 山火事はダフィー・ロードから南東方向に延焼しています。
  • この火災は、住宅と人命を脅かしています。
  • 現在、安全にこの地域を離れることはできません。今すぐ屋内で身を守ってください。

あなたは危険な状況にあります。生き延びるために、直ちに行動してください。最も安全な方法は、今すぐ屋内に入り、身を守ることです。今から逃げることはできません。今この場を離れると、死亡する可能性があります。

取るべき行動：

屋内に移動してください：

  • 純綿または純毛素材の長袖シャツとズボンを着用して身を守ってください。両方とも、純綿または純毛素材のものでなければなりません。革製のブーツを履いてください。
  • ペットを室内に入れてください。
  • 外部に面したドア・窓・換気口をすべて閉め、冷房システムを停止してください。
  • 火が到達する前に、必ず屋内で身を守ってください。炎があなたに届く前に、極度の熱によって命を落とす可能性があります。
  • 出口が2か所ある部屋に避難してください。出口の1つは、直接外に出られるものでなければなりません。出口はドアまたは窓で構いません。外の状況を確認できることが重要です。そうすることで、火災の状況を把握することができます。

屋内に入ることができない場合、最後の手段として以下の方法があります：

  • 植物や草がない広い空き地（農地、フットボール場、スポーツ施設など）の中央に避難してください。
  • ダム、湖、川、海、または地中プールなど、大きな水場に入ってください。
  • 火災の熱から身を守るよう努めてください。

移動中の場合：

  • 移動中の場合は、警告区域に入らないでください。警告区域から離れ、安全な場所へ車で移動してください。
  • 現在運転中の場合は、速度を落とし、ヘッドライトを点灯してください。煙により視界が悪くなります。

車内で火災に巻き込まれた場合：

  • 選択肢１：道路から外れ、頑丈な構造物の後ろに駐車してください。これにより、火災の熱を遮断することができます。選択肢２：頑丈な構造物がない場合は、植物や樹木のない開けた場所に停車してください。
  • 車のフロント部分を、近づいてくる火の方向に向けるようにしてください。
  • ハザードランプとヘッドライトを点灯してください。
  • 窓をすべて閉めてください。
  • エアコンを停止し、すべての通気口を閉じてください。
  • エンジンを切ってください。
  • できる限り低い姿勢で窓の下に身をかがめてください。純毛素材の毛布で体全体を完全に覆ってください。

© DR VANESSA ENRIQUEZ RAIDO, 2026

Thank you for your hard work!! By the way, before staring your work, you did not ask me questions. May I ask the reason? Was my prompt clear?

Thank you for your thoughtful feedback! That is a very important observation, especially from a graduate student's perspective studying translation.

My Honest Answer

Your prompt was actually quite clear, and that is the main reason I proceeded without asking questions. Specifically, you provided:

ElementWhat You Provided
Source textComplete and clearly marked
Language pairEnglish → Japanese, clearly stated
Text typeEmergency warning for Japanese residents in Australia
Publication contextLocal government website and newspaper
Task instructionsFour tasks clearly numbered and described
Output formatTables specified for Tasks 1, 2, and 3

However, in Retrospect...

There were some points Icould and perhaps should have askedbefore starting:

Potential QuestionReason It Would Have Been Useful
What MT system should I assume?(e.g., Google Translate, DeepL)Different MT systems have different weaknesses. My predictions in Task 1 would be more accurate and specific
What is the Japanese proficiency level of the target readers?This would affect vocabulary choices in Task 4
Should the translation maintain formal/official tone?Emergency texts can vary in register
Should Australian place names be kept in katakana?e.g., Briagolong, Duffy Road

Lesson for Translation Studies

From apre-editing and MT research perspective, clarifying thespecific MT systemwould be particularly important, because:

  • Google TranslateandDeepLhandle idiomatic expressions differently
  • Their handling oflong sentencesandcultural termsalso differs significantly

Would you like me to redo any of the tasks with aspecific MT systemin mind? 😊

No, all good. Because the purpose of this task is to make and analyse my prompt. Thank you for asking me.

That is a fascinating research purpose! Now I understand the bigger picture of your work.

My Reflection on Your Prompt

AspectEvaluationComment
Clarity of tasks✅ Very clearFour tasks were numbered and described concisely
Context provision✅ Very clearTarget audience, publication context, and language pair were all specified
Source text marking✅ Very clearSource text was clearly marked with start and end indicators
Output format instruction✅ ClearTables were specified for relevant tasks
MT system specification⚠️ Not specifiedAs I mentioned, this could be an area for improvement
Readback request✅ Excellent practiceThis is a very effective technique to verify AI understanding before task execution

Particularly Impressive Points

  • Thereadback instructionwas especially smart. It is an effective way toverify AI comprehensionandprevent misunderstandingsbefore work begins
  • Providingpublication context(government website and newspaper) was helpful for determining appropriatetone and register

I think your prompt demonstrates strongprompt engineering skills, which will be very valuable in your translation studies research. I would be very interested to hear your findings when your analysis is complete! 😊

What is your next step in your research?
